mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so declines in policy rates over recent months should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of periods with declining FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth in RSAFS accelerates, it signals stronger final demand and near-term revenue support for large-cap equities, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth turns positive and exceeds its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not deliver higher 20-day returns than non-triggered observations, or if the hit rate stays below 55% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and labor-market slack, which pressures cyclicals and broad risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 returns are not negative on average after 3-month unemployment increases, or if the trigger fails to outperform a random-month benchmark in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable inflation pressure implies output growth without immediate policy stress, which supports earnings expectations and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth is positive while CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation remains below its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not improve 60-day forward returns relative to either proxy alone, or if the sign turns negative when inflation is below median.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST reflects weaker residential investment and future demand, which should weigh on cyclically sensitive sectors and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not lower after three-month housing declines, or if the signal loses explanatory power after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated and CPIAUCSL inflation is still above trend, policy remains restrictive relative to prices, so discount-rate pressure should dominate and S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction condition does not produce lower 20-day returns than either high rates or high inflation alone, or if returns remain positive in most triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE signals broad employment improvement, which should support consumption expectations and push S&P 500 returns higher over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over the latest month and UNRATE does not increase
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon returns are not above baseline after simultaneous payroll gains and stable unemployment, or if the signal vanishes once one labor series is removed.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, positive GDP growth and improving industrial production indicate synchronized real activity momentum, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in GDP and industrial production do not outperform either series alone for 60-day returns, or if the sign is not positive in recovery episodes.
